summary: Ukrainian drones struck the Volgograd Lukoil refinery, halting its 15 million‑ton annual output. Russia intensified guided‑bomb and Shahed attacks across multiple oblasts while redeploying MiG‑31K aircraft and preparing Kinzhal launches. Both sides report significant equipment losses and escalating air‑defense challenges.
key_points: Ukrainian drones attacked the Volgograd Lukoil refinery, stopping processing of ~15 Mt of oil per year (Source: @UkraineNow/81605).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs on Dnipropetrovsk, Sumy, Donetsk, and Zaporizhzhia regions (Source: @kpszsu/63675, @kpszsu/63690, @kpszsu/63662).

Multiple Shahed (loitering munition) impacts reported near Kurenevka, Lukiantsi, Podil, and other villages in Kyiv region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67427, @mon1tor_ua/67426, @mon1tor_ua/67425, @mon1tor_ua/67448).
Russia claims a stockpile of over 200 Tsirkon hypersonic missiles aimed at Kyiv, stating they can reach the capital in six minutes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30614).
Ukrainian UAV strike damaged a locomotive in Kharkiv region, injuring a railway worker (Source: @UkraineNow/81616).
Russian forces struck a Fire Point facility in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast that produced UAV components (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30613).
MiG‑31K aircraft were observed relocating and potential Kinzhal missile launches were announced (Source: @eRadarrua/95026).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian drones halted output at the major Volgograd Lukoil refinery. Russia responded with a wave of guided‑bomb strikes across Dnipropetrovsk, Sumy, Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia, and intensified Shahed attacks on Kyiv‑region villages. Both sides are mobilising air assets, including MiG‑31K redeployments and claimed Tsirkon missile stockpiles, indicating heightened operational pressure.

points: Ukrainian UAVs hit the Volgograd Lukoil refinery on 29 May, stopping processing of roughly 15 million tonnes of oil annually, a key southern Russian asset (Source: @UkraineNow/81605).
A UAV strike on a locomotive in Kharkiv region injured a railway worker, though train traffic continued with minimal delays (Source: @UkraineNow/81616).
Ukrainian forces reported a fire at the Fire Point facility in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast, which manufactures long‑range UAV components, after a Russian strike (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30613).
Multiple UAV sightings were reported near Piriatyn, Priluki, Dnipropetrovsk, and Chaplynyn, indicating continued reconnaissance activity (Sources: @kpszsu/63645, @kpszsu/63677, @eRadarrua/95002).

Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aviation bombs on Dnipropetrovsk, Sumy, Donetsk, and Zaporizhzhia oblasts, targeting both military and civilian infrastructure (Sources: @kpszsu/63675, @kpszsu/63690, @kpszsu/63662).
Russia announced the relocation of two Tu‑95MS bombers that participated in a recent Far‑East strike, confirming none are currently armed with missiles (Source: @eRadarrua/95020).
MiG‑31K fighters were observed moving from Olenya to Savasleyka airfield, with potential Kinzhal missile launches mentioned (Source: @eRadarrua/95026).
Russian forces claimed to have launched a large number of Shahed loitering munitions, with ongoing launches reported throughout the night (Source: @UkraineNow/81604).
Ukrainian drones were observed over Piriatyn and Priluki, moving from the southeast, suggesting reconnaissance near the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/63645).
Guided bomb attacks by Russian tactical aviation targeted Dnipropetrovsk and Sumy regions, key areas of the eastern front (Sources: @kpszsu/63675, @kpszsu/63690).
A fire at the Fire Point facility in Dnipropetrovsk, a producer of UAV components, indicates Russian attempts to degrade Ukrainian drone capabilities (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30613).
Russian Shahed attacks were reported near Lukiantsi and Kurenevka, villages close to the front line, causing local damage (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67427).
UAV sightings reported on the northern side of Zhytomyr, Kyiv, and Chernihiv regions, moving westward, indicating surveillance activity (Source: @kpszsu/63672).
A new UAV was spotted heading toward Horodnya in northern Chernihiv Oblast (Source: @eRadarrua/95009).
Russian tactical aviation announced activity on north‑eastern and eastern directions, warning of possible strikes near front‑line areas (Source: @kpszsu/63671).
MiG‑31K aircraft were observed in the north, relocating between airfields, suggesting a shift in air‑defence posture (Source: @eRadarrua/95026).
Ukrainian drones struck the Volgograd Lukoil refinery in southern Russia, halting a major oil‑processing plant (Source: @UkraineNow/81605).
Russian guided‑bomb attacks were launched on Zaporizhzhia Oblast, a key southern front region (Source: @kpszsu/63662).
Shahed attacks were reported near the southern village of Alieksievo in Belgorod region, causing fires at a melzavod facility (Source: @eRadarrua/94973).
Russian forces claimed a large Shahed launch wave, with ongoing attacks across multiple southern oblasts (Source: @UkraineNow/81604).
Russia deployed guided aviation bombs across four oblasts, indicating intensified air‑strike capability (Sources: @kpszsu/63675, @kpszsu/63690, @kpszsu/63662).
MiG‑31K fighters were observed relocating and potential Kinzhal missile launches were announced, showing high‑speed interceptor activity (Source: @eRadarrua/95026).

Ukrainian UAV activity was reported over multiple regions, including Dnipropetrovsk, Kharkiv, and northern oblasts, reflecting ongoing reconnaissance and strike missions (Sources: @kpszsu/63645, @kpszsu/63677, @eRadarrua/95002).
Russian Shahed loitering munition attacks continued across Kyiv‑region villages, highlighting persistent low‑altitude threats (Sources: @mon1tor_ua/67427, @mon1tor_ua/67426).

key_developments: Ukrainian drones halted processing at the Volgograd Lukoil refinery, stopping ~15 Mt of oil annually (Source: @UkraineNow/81605).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs on Dnipropetrovsk, Sumy, Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/63675, @kpszsu/63690, @kpszsu/63662).

Multiple Shahed loitering munitions impacted villages in Kyiv region, including Kurenevka, Lukiantsi, Podil and Borisivka (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67427, @mon1tor_ua/67426, @mon1tor_ua/67425, @mon1tor_ua/67448).
Russia claims a stockpile of over 200 Tsirkon hypersonic missiles capable of reaching Kyiv in six minutes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30614).
Ukrainian UAV strike damaged a locomotive in Kharkiv region, injuring a railway worker (Source: @UkraineNow/81616).
Russian forces struck the Fire Point facility in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast, a producer of long‑range UAV components (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30613).
MiG‑31K aircraft were observed relocating with potential Kinzhal missile launches announced (Source: @eRadarrua/95026).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian air operations have escalated, employing guided bombs across multiple fronts and redeploying high‑speed interceptors capable of launching Tsirkon and Kinzhal missiles. Ukrainian UAV activity continues to target strategic Russian infrastructure, notably the Volgograd refinery, while Shahed loitering munitions threaten civilian areas in the Kyiv region. The combined effect raises the risk of rapid, high‑intensity strikes and strains Ukrainian air‑defence resources.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine has heightened rail‑safety monitoring and continued civilian assistance after UAV attacks, while Russian officials cite large‑scale Shahed launches as justification for intensified air campaigns. International observers note the strategic significance of the Volgograd refinery strike and the reported Tsirkon stockpile, potentially influencing diplomatic pressure on Russia.
sections.intelligence_notes: Future monitoring should focus on the movement of MiG‑31K units and any confirmed Kinzhal launches, as well as the operational status of the Volgograd refinery post‑attack. Tracking Shahed launch patterns in the Kyiv region will aid civilian protection measures. Verification of the claimed Tsirkon stockpile remains essential for assessing missile threat levels.
